title: microcybe multiflora subsp. multiflora
intro: Microcybe multiflora subsp. multiflora, also known as the Red False Unicorn, is a perennial plant species in the Asclepiadaceae family, native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its root, which contains compounds that have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Microcybe multiflora subsp. multiflora include relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the root, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal use.
